hypothesis: Incorporating explicit metal coordination environment descriptors—such as coordination number, oxidation state, and ligand field geometry—as auxiliary input features during fine-tuning of uMLIPs on out-of-equilibrium MOF configurations will yield greater accuracy improvements in structural optimization and bulk modulus prediction than equivalent increases in training dataset size alone, because the primary failure mode of current uMLIPs on MOFs is misrepresentation of coordination bond directionality rather than insufficient coverage of the potential energy surface.
